nodes.title: Define control-character stripping
nodes.why: Attackers can hide dangerous schemes with invisible control characters.
nodes.tradeoff: Stronger normalization, with a tiny extra string pass.

nodes.title: Use a strict scheme allowlist
nodes.why: For `href`, allowlisting known-safe protocols is safer than trying to block all bad ones.
nodes.alternative: Block only `javascript:` and allow everything else.
nodes.tradeoff: Safer default behavior, but it intentionally rejects unknown schemes.

nodes.title: Reject null and empty input early
nodes.why: Early guards keep the sanitizer predictable and avoid broken links.
nodes.alternative: Return empty string for empty input.
nodes.tradeoff: Clear unsafe signal (`null`) instead of silent empty href behavior.

nodes.title: Normalize before policy checks
nodes.why: All later checks should run on cleaned input, not raw user text.
nodes.alternative: Check schemes before stripping control chars.
nodes.tradeoff: Better detection reliability with minimal overhead.

nodes.title: Block protocol-relative and backslash URLs
nodes.why: These forms can lead to surprising external navigation or parsing issues.
nodes.alternative: Allow `//` links and rely on browser behavior.
nodes.tradeoff: Safer navigation behavior, but stricter than some apps expect.

nodes.title: Allow common relative navigation patterns
nodes.why: Most internal app links are relative and should remain usable.
nodes.tradeoff: Better UX for SPAs, while still filtering unsafe protocols.

nodes.title: Detect explicit scheme, keep plain paths
nodes.why: If no scheme exists, values like `profile` can be treated as safe relative paths.
nodes.alternative: Reject anything without an explicit scheme.
nodes.tradeoff: More user-friendly paths, while still enforcing scheme rules.

nodes.title: Reject non-allowlisted protocols
nodes.why: This is the key XSS barrier against `javascript:`, `data:`, and similar protocols.
nodes.alternative: Maintain a long denylist of dangerous schemes.
nodes.tradeoff: Allowlist is simpler and safer, but intentionally restrictive.

nodes.title: Parse and normalize http/https using URL
nodes.why: Using `URL` catches malformed links and returns a normalized href string.
nodes.alternative: Return raw string after allowlist check.
nodes.tradeoff: More robust validation, with a parse step and try/catch.

nodes.title: Return mailto/tel as-is after checks
nodes.why: After normalization and allowlist checks, these schemes are already considered safe.
nodes.tradeoff: Simple and practical, but does not enforce extra format validation.
